reviews.review_msg: this is a dry class with lots of writing assignments. The lectures are 3 hours long which is alot especially for a statistic course. All exam prep material were for the first 5 lectures, we had no practice questions for the other 11 lecs. Marking takes too long, going into the final, 50% of material wasn't graded yet

(BPK409)Starts with a little tutorial on coding that doesn't prepare you at all for the course. Tells you to figure out the coding yourself when you always run into problems because of outdated lab instructions. She and TA rarely give a direct answer but always give attitude. No final exam but a final oral and big project due a week after classes.

she starts the course (BPK 409) by saying "I'm going to assume you have no coding experience" then proceeds to never help you with coding. BPK 409 is a course where you teach yourself concepts that engineering students will tell you that you have no business learning for a basic coding class. she is very nice, but a terrible prof for 409. avoid.

Took a stats course with her, and although statistics is usually a pretty dry class to take, she made it enjoyable. Explained topics well, provided detailed videos for completing the labs, and was pretty positive/enthusiastic the entire semester.

Diana is super caring and a great lecturer. Fair exams and quizzes. However, the projects dented my grade, which is disappointing considering how much work/time I put into them. By part 3 of the assignment, I was super stressed and gave up. My grade was in the A range before the project and now I'm at B+...so my efforts feel a bit wasted.

I've taken several of Diana's classes and have enjoyed them. I particularly enjoyed her teaching 340. She does have a voice that puts you to sleep at 8:30a, but she's good at breaking lectures up into group activities that are related to the final. Can be a difficult grader, exams based on lectures and readings. Really well presented OL courses.

Unbelievable guy. If you pay attention and really conceptualize the class, it is easy and is very interesting to everyday life. This isn't a kickback class, but it is also not super difficult. Take your time on assignments and start early. Stephen is a very fair and respected guy. Very positive experience being taught by him.

His marking scheme is super subjective and course material is overall irrelevant to the field. Sure he's a nice guy but his slides are poorly presented and the "textbook" is full of errors. Basically a course where you memorize lists that won't help you in the future.
good thing this senile man is retiring, marks way too hard, not fair at all, dissapear stephen

His book is poorly written. Typos, lack of knowledge of the millennial era, irrelevant information, and he paraphrases articles so poorly that I'd rather just read the article it self. Lectures are almost a joke. Goes over like 10 slides in 2.5 hours. Take out the stretches and one break,  we'd be gone in 2 hours max including in class activity.

Honestly, the most enjoyable class you'll take in BPK upper division. This class is hilarious and you also learn quite a bit.  Stephen Brown does an awesome job keeping the class engaged and is very fair with grades. Take it with him!

His class is quite easy and even though its 3hrs long, he usually ends it in one and a half hours. He doesn't teach much in class so you really need the textbook for the concept and because the questions are right from the textbook. He also takes stretch breaks every few minutes too so it's never really boring. It's the only class I never slept in.

Peter sets a true example for a scholar, and inspires you to be better in every aspect of your life. The design of the course was fair and allowed students to take charge of their learning. There's a big focus on group work, and there isn't excessive direction given for the assignments. They allow you to show creativity. Amazing!!!

I think Jim taught a fair class, there was a lot of complaining about the midterm but I thought it was very fair. The grading of the final presentations was more than fair and I would say he was VERY lenient. He is a nice guy and he cares which is always good in a professor. I think what you put in you get out.

Important class, executed horribly. Overall, great prof but not for this class. The only thing that helped develop any applicable skills was the group presentation. The midterm and final were straight memorization of irrelevant info from random slides. Lectures were really boring, long, and not useful, but attendance was required.

cut the guy some slack because 340 is hard to teach, but this is actually horrendous. hundreds of slides of rote memory rather than more emphasis on actually applying course concepts in case study based scenarios. class should've been more project focus, more assignments, and not like a traditional bpk course. this shouldn't be in the curriculum
